title: senecio vulgaris var. crassifolius
intro: Senecio vulgaris var. crassifolius, also known as broad-leaved groundsel, is a variety of the common groundsel plant. It is a flowering plant that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often found growing in disturbed or waste areas. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and is used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to aid in digestion and reduce fever.
